text.p: Cancellation of Severe Thunderstorm Warning - Adelaide Region
Adelaide Hills and Adelaide council areas.
The State Emergency Service advises that people should:
* Keep clear of fallen power lines.
* Beware of fallen trees or debris on the road.
* Don't drive, ride or walk through flood water.
* Keep clear of creeks and storm drains.

Check https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts. Warnings are also available through TV and Radio broadcasts or call 1300 659 210. The Bureau and State Emergency Service would appreciate warnings being broadcast regularly.

forecast-period.text: Heavy rainfall has eased throughout the Adelaide region

text.p: A more general severe thunderstorm warning remains current for parts of the Eastern Eyre Peninsula, Flinders, North West Pastoral and North East Pastoral districts.
Severe thunderstorms are no longer affecting the Adelaide Region. This warning is now cancelled.
28 mm was recorded at Glenelg in the 30 minutes to 8:00 pm.
27.8 mm was recorded at Patawalonga Lake in the 30 minutes to 8:00 pm.
24.6 mm was recorded at Adelaide Airport in the 30 minutes to 8:00 pm.

hazard.text: cancellation of severe thunderstorm warning - adelaide region

text.p: Severe thunderstorms are no longer affecting the Adelaide Region. This warning is now cancelled.
28 mm was recorded at Glenelg in the 30 minutes to 8:00 pm.
27.8 mm was recorded at Patawalonga Lake in the 30 minutes to 8:00 pm.
24.6 mm was recorded at Adelaide Airport in the 30 minutes to 8:00 pm.
